items.title: Clop ransomware targets Windchill, FlexPLM in data theft attacks
items.author: Sergiu Gatlan
items.content: <article>The Clop ransomware gang (also tracked as Cl0p) is targeting Internet-exposed PTC Windchill and FlexPLM instances in a new data theft extortion campaign. [...]</article>

items.title: New Dolphin X malware uses AI to rank high-value targets
items.author: Lawrence Abrams
items.content: <article>A new Dolphin X remote access trojan claims to use an AI-powered profiling feature to score and rank infected users, helping cybercriminals identify which victims should be targeted first. [...]</article>

items.title: Australian energy provider Origin says data breach exposes client data
items.author: Bill Toulas
items.content: <article>Origin Energy has confirmed that an unauthorized party accessed and subsequently leaked customer data online, exposing sensitive personally identifiable information (PII), among others. [...]</article>

items.title: Fake Claude app promoted by Bing ads pushes SectopRAT malware
items.author: Bill Toulas
items.content: <article>A malvertising campaign on the Bing search service is pushing a fake Claude desktop app installer hosted on a legitimate Claude.ai domain to deliver the SectopRAT malware. [...]</article>

items.title: Russian hackers exploit Zimbra zero-click flaw for email theft
items.author: Lawrence Abrams
items.content: <article>CISA is warning that the Russian state-sponsored hacking group Laundry Bear, also known as Void Blizzard, is targeting organizations using Zimbra Collaboration email servers by combining phishing attacks with the exploitation of a now-patched Zimbra vulnerability. [...]</article>

items.title: Hackers abuse Notepad++ plugins to stealthily install malware
items.author: Bill Toulas
items.content: <article>Ukraine's CERT has uncovered attacks distributing an archive containing the legitimate Notepad++ application and a malicious utility called LunchPoke disguised as a plugin to establish persistence. [...]</article>

items.title: Microsoft 365 outage affects Teams, SharePoint and other services
items.author: Mayank Parmar
items.content: <article>Microsoft is impacted by a massive outage affecting Teams and Microsoft 365 services, primarily affecting users in North America. [...]</article>

items.title: FedRAMP Rev5 Is Ending: What the 20x Transition Really Requires
items.author: Sponsored by Anecdotes
items.content: <article>FedRAMP 20X replaces point-in-time assessments with continuous, machine-readable evidence that demonstrates security controls are working. Anecdotes explains what the transition from Rev5 to FedRAMP 20X means and how organizations can prepare for continuous, evidence-based assurance. [...]</article>

items.title: EU fines Google $1 billion for search, app store antitrust violations
items.author: Sergiu Gatlan
items.content: <article>The European Commission fined Google €890 million ($1 billion) on Thursday after finding the company had violated the European Union's Digital Markets Act (DMA), which ensures fair online competition. [...]</article>

items.title: New RefluXFS Linux flaw lets attackers gain root privileges
items.author: Sergiu Gatlan
items.content: <article>A nine-year-old race condition vulnerability in the Linux kernel's XFS filesystem, tracked as CVE-2026-64600, allows local attackers to overwrite protected files and gain root privileges. [...]</article>

items.title: New msaRAT malware uses Chrome, Edge browsers to route C2 traffic
items.author: Bill Toulas
items.content: <article>The Chaos ransomware gang is using a new backdoor dubbed msaRAT that hides command-and-control (C2) communication by routing it through the Chrome or Edge browsers. [...]</article>

items.title: Microsoft working to fix Exchange Online mailbox quarantine issue
items.author: Sergiu Gatlan
items.content: <article>Microsoft is working to resolve an ongoing Exchange Online issue that has been mistakenly quarantining customers' mailboxes since Sunday. [...]</article>

items.title: Check Point warns of SmartConsole zero-day exploited in attacks
items.author: Sergiu Gatlan
items.content: <article>Israeli cybersecurity firm Check Point Software has addressed an actively exploited zero-day flaw in the company's SmartConsole graphical user interface (GUI) admin panel. [...]</article>

items.title: Upbound says hack caused $13 million in fraudulent Acima leases
items.author: Bill Toulas
items.content: <article>The Upbound Group fintech company disclosed that threat actors who stole data from its systems leveraged it to create $13 million in Acima leases. [...]</article>

items.title: South Korea discloses data breach impacting diplomats worldwide
items.author: Bill Toulas
items.content: <article>South Korea disclosed that hackers breached the National Diplomatic Academy's online education system for ten months and stole personal information belonging to current and former employees of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs (MFA), including overseas diplomats. [...]</article>
